phases.why: At level 3 the numbers lean his way — the windwall dash-weaving out-tick your pool-drain sustain in even exchanges. Don't bleed for pride; take the trades his cooldowns hand you.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Slight deficit through levels 4-5: Yasuo's windwall dash-weaving edge out your pool-drain sustain on the margins. You can still trade — just let him start the exchanges and answer on your terms.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: Hemoplague online — the all-in inverts: Q-E-R-E with ignite kills most mids who thought they were winning.

phases.label: First item
phases.why: Slight deficit on first item: Yasuo's windwall dash-weaving edge out your pool-drain sustain on the margins. You can still trade — just let him start the exchanges and answer on your terms.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: Late Vlad is a raid boss: pool their engage, R their clump, and drain whatever stayed to argue.

phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: Q-farm and nothing else — your level 1 is among the worst mid. Give CS you can't Q safely and smile about it.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: Still surviving. Q on cooldown when free, pool saved for the jungler you haven't seen yet.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: E charges join the pattern: Q-E wave contact trades start mattering. Health is your mana — spend like an accountant.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: The crimson rush timing — Q empowers every third-ish cast and the sustain war starts tilting your way.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: Hemoplague online — the all-in inverts: Q-E-R-E with ignite kills most mids who thought they were winning.

phases.label: First item
phases.why: Riftmaker timing: every trade heals more than it costs. Extended fights are now donations to your health bar.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: Late Vlad is a raid boss: pool their engage, R their clump, and drain whatever stayed to argue.
